metadata.author: Zephaniah
metadata.subject: Jehovah’s Judgment on the People of God and on the Nations, and His Salvation for the Remnant of His People.

1: The word of Jehovah that came to Zephaniah the son of Cushi... in the days of Josiah the son of Amon, king of Judah.
The great day of Jehovah is near; it is near and hastens greatly! The sound of the day of Jehovah is bitter; the mighty man cries out there.
Neither their silver nor their gold will be able to deliver them in the day of the wrath of Jehovah.
2: Seek Jehovah, all you lowly of the earth who have performed His ordinance; seek righteousness, seek humility. Perhaps you will be hidden in the day of the anger of Jehovah.
3: For then I will change the language of the peoples to a pure language, that all of them may call upon the name of Jehovah, to serve Him with one accord.
The King of Israel, Jehovah, is in your midst; you will not see evil anymore.
Jehovah your God is in your midst, a mighty One who will save; He will rejoice over you with gladness; He will rest in His love; He will exult over you with a ringing shout.

14.text: The day of Jehovah is a recurring theme in the prophets, referring to the time when God intervenes directly in human history to judge evil and establish His reign.

9.text: This signifies the reversal of the confusion of Babel, where a unified and purified expression is restored to humanity to worship the true God.

17.text: A profound expression of God's intimate satisfaction in His redeemed people; He is so settled in His love for them that He finds silent rest and loud joy in their presence.
